framework.name: GNAP Is Not OAuth Extension — Clean-Sheet Redesign
framework.description: GNAP explicitly breaks compatibility with OAuth 2.0, meaning it's a conscious re-architecture of the constrained delegation primitive, not incremental. This matters because it suggests the IETF community recognized OAuth's model was insufficient for the agent-delegation pattern and rebuilt from scratch.
framework.raw_quote: GNAP is not an extension of OAuth 2.0 and is not intended to be directly compatible with OAuth 2.0. GNAP seeks to provide functionality and solve use cases that OAuth 2.0 cannot easily or cleanly address.

source.title: RFC 9635 - Grant Negotiation and Authorization Protocol (GNAP)
